morning.notes: Different route — took Basho west toward Sloans Lake instead of the usual north loop. He was confused for one block and then completely fine. Good reminder.

work.notes: Pipeline review with my manager. She mentioned the fast track conversation again — Senior AE, more accounts, travel three weeks a month. She said she wanted to know my timeline. I said I was thinking about it. This is the third time she has mentioned it in six weeks.

fast_track.honest_note: The honest thing is that I don't not want it. I also don't want my life to be 60% airports. I don't know how to hold both of those at once.

debt.notes: Down $600 from last check. Slow. The commission variance from last month was smaller than I expected — deal pushed to next quarter. Still on pace for under $22K by end of Q3, but less margin than I'd like.

camera.notes: I defined the project today. Actually defined it. Saturday mornings, the coffee shop, the same table, whoever is sitting there, for one year. I wrote it down. The project exists now.

portland.notes: I booked it. $280 round trip. Done.

evening.dinner: Cooked properly — the salmon I've been avoiding attempting. It was better than I expected.
evening.reading: Read 40 pages of the boat book. The man is beginning to understand why he started building it, which took him 80 pages to figure out. I recognize this.

wins: Portland booked — for real this time
Camera project defined and written down
at_risk: Fast track decision — can't defer indefinitely; manager will want an answer
note: The project exists now that it's written down. That's how I know I mean it.
